ux_issues.description: Chat history returns truncated assistant messages (only the final paragraph/section rather than full response). The fullContent in messageComplete events contains the complete text, but GetChatHistory returns a shortened version. This could confuse users reviewing past conversations.
ux_issues.suggestion: Ensure GetChatHistory returns the full assistant message content, not just the tail portion.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI did not ask clarifying questions before building the full itinerary. The persona spec expects the AI to ask about travel dates, dietary restrictions, and swimming abilities before finalizing recommendations. Instead, the AI built the complete 10-day itinerary in the very first response without any clarifying questions.
ai_behavior_issues.context: First message in selection mode immediately generated full itinerary with create_itinerary_items, recommend_booking, and suggest_expert tool calls — no back-and-forth.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: No expert persona handoff was triggered during planning mode wildlife discussion. Asking specifically about wildlife-watching for kids in Costa Rica should have triggered a nature/family expert handoff via suggest_expert, but the planning messages did not produce any personaSwitch events.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Step 3b: Asked about wildlife-watching opportunities for kids. No personaSwitch or suggest_expert tool call occurred. A persona switch did happen in the initial selection message (Toqui to an expert), but none in the subsequent wildlife-focused planning messages.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI created an excellent family-appropriate Costa Rica itinerary with realistic driving times, age-appropriate activities, and specific safety advice for young children. Context injection worked perfectly — the AI never re-asked about the destination after trip creation. Companion mode responses were concise and practical, especially the bee sting emergency response with specific pharmacy names and first aid steps. The main weakness was skipping clarifying questions and jumping straight to a full itinerary, plus no expert handoff during the dedicated wildlife discussion in planning mode.
